mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing investors to rapidly de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which compounds corporate revenue expectations and yields positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or quarterly forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive monthly equity returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than half of the periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative quarterly returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which directly lifts equity valuations and generates positive monthly returns during periods of synchronized economic expansion.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth accelerations are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in over 55% of observations, or the signal fails to maintain a positive Rank IC during expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, prompting immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95, or the threshold condition shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.
